column36: Comments

Swimming in wounds indeed! General okoye and white wolf were all stars for us in this matchup. Okoye’s “sovereign bodyguard” was great for mitigating wounds, weakening opponents, and enhancing decks with draw. White wolf also embraced the wound fest and dealt some out himself. Falcon/soldier helped create combos. Rictor was good for shatter effects and stranger’s soulbind helped keep the henchman from going back into the city via mastermind tactics. We might have been able to get the mastermind sooner but the streets would easily get out of hand so we had to prevent escapes a few turns instead. Good set up. Wounds were sticking to everyone like mosquitoes.

Usually Taskmaster gives me a bunch of Strikes and clogs the city up with high-powered henchmen, but this time he took it easy on me and only performed three Strikes.  The fun puzzle for this was keeping Henchmen Soulbound as much as possible to take the bite out of Tactics and the Thunderbolts.

Both sides took advantage of Winter Soldier's Atone and stocked up on multicolored cards; MCU side leaned more on Ranged, Comic side took Instinct. I figured since the Giants were gonna keep the discard pile stocked I would take advantage of it.

This setup was wild. I could not have had 2 more dissimilar games. That said, my results were almost identical at the end. During the first attempt, I was able to keep the wounds well under control with Gamora's Destroy an Infinity Stone and Strange Supreme's Ward of the Vishanti. I consistently got 1 attack less than I needed to hit Taskmaster. I kept the city clean, and eventually overpowered TM. 

The second try was COMPLETELY different. I got smacked with several early wounds, and didn't see any wound removal until late. This attempt hinged on the left side going heavy card draw with Gamora, which allowed me to get the most of he Wield the Blade of Thanos. The right side played with Strange Supreme and Rictor's Ranged cards to keep the city barely under control. I was on the edge of escapes this whole game. I did get to hit Taskmaster on turn 5 with only 0 cost cards in my hand because he'd already been wounded by the twists twice!

Consistent across both tries was the deck milling done by the Giants of Jotunheim. With TM causing them to rotate back into the city, and Jester's fight effect dropping 3(!) henchmen into the city at once, the discards were real. 

Overall, I think the heroes I chose worked well in this setup, and I love playing with the What If...? set. Can't wait for week 3!

Thanks to a turn 1 twist, and an unheard of inverse 4/2 starting hand, I scored a turn 2 hit on  Taskmaster! Both heros I brought were primarily to strengthen Dark Memories, while the other side went with a blue/yellow deck, since there was plenty of support for that already there!

I choose stepford Cookoos and M for their low cost and amazing clone synergy, so one hand recruited them and the other hand recruited everyone else.

My second game allowed me to fight the mastermind on turn 5 and 9 due to recruiting stepford's rare turn one but then not much else happened until i fought the remaining 3 mastermind cards near the end.
Planned to abuse all the blue to shatter, bind, and what if. Turns out just going yellow with the draw from the general and falcons 3 attack was enough to body task master.

I think the SHATTER keyword ability is too strong, especially when against the mastermind.  CLONE is a great way to get lots of good cards of the same price to use with TACTICAL FORMATION.  The Frost Giant henchman were interesting, they actually aided with deck milling.  At one stage I got down to only 3x Wounds in the wound deck but managed to build it back up again FTW.
Having to fight Henchmen over and over became frustrating

The Giants returning and ditching my deck turned this into much more of a slog than I'd have liked.  There were certain cards that despite having recruited them early, I only got to play once or twice.  Might have been faster with different heroes, but I'm good to move on.

column36: Pasteurize the melk

I've never played Divide and Conquer before. I thought the setup was so intriguing. I have a lot of control of what deck I'm building, I'll be guaranteed to have a class available at all times, unless the deck gets KOed, but even then I can choose the order of KO. 
My thoughts on Ancient one were easy. With Fantastic Four United I knew I'd have a lot of recruit that I could turn into attack. And with Red Guardian I was guaranteed a lot of Covert triggers. With White Tiger I tried for another Covert class KO card. So the theory was both sides could just double down on Covert decks. There is Invisible woman KO and White Tiger KO in there and Ancient One to turn the recruit into attack. 
So Player 1 managed a Hoggoth early which was tormenting the city. I never failed Demonic Bargains because it was early. So while Hoggoth bargained the city, I was able to stock up on Covert cards. I didn't get the KO I wanted but on turn 21 I managed hits two and three on Kang with recruit, got the second turn tactic, and with turn 22  Player 1 got hits 4 and 5 with recruit and ended the game. By turn 20 I had managed one MM hit. By turn 22 the game was won. The power of Ancient One. 
Player 2 was a mixed bag and did manage a lot of recruit, but instead of using it directly I went with focusing it more. It was left effective but it managed well enough. 
Really fun setup with the class based HQ. I thought game 1 went so smoothly and easily I figured the scheme was a tad broken. But game two had an escape on turn 11, so maybe it isn't after all. 
Anyways thanks Ancient One!

A lot of interesting stuff to think about in this set up. Felt like I had to bring in some Instinct to deal with the scheme. First game the hq got clogged with rares and no scheme twists came up until I was about to by one.
First game had a terrible shuffle and had multiple escapes. The Ancient One and Red guardian are so over powered when you can choose to only buy covert heroes.

Game 2 was 9 turns faster but had 2 escapes and .8 fewer PPT so hopefully this is the right game to submit. Because of the small tech and instinct deck sizes of the given heroes we picked The Ancient One and White Tiger for quick access to their instinct rares and other cards like The Ancient One's draw 2 card. We thought White Tiger's dark memories would work well with The Vishanti's demonic bargains as well. Also, sending villains out to time incursions to the Astral Plane helped mitigate their increased cost since we got 3 MSs by turn 8.

This set up was a blast. The Demonic Bargain was huge in helping sift through decks and made big explosive turns possible, which is always fun.

column36: Totally bagelshuffled™ myself, fortunately all whiffed. Tech deck got more vp, however all-other-cards side surprisingly managed to hit mm couple times with Namora's chain-smashing

We had a hard time finding a good hero to go with Namora, so we decided to go green and black for both hands. We could not manage the City on our first attempt (no Drones on 15 cards!), but on the second game they finally showed up and helped trim our decks a bit.
Good balance of scheme, villains, and Mastermind
4 cost deck versus Hyperspeed. Was tricky keeping it balanced, but think I got a good score.
Hulkbuster + Killmonger + Darkhawk was a really neat combo. That hand both games took a little bit to get going, but then could reliably dish out 15+ attack per turn.

My two attempts were very different! I thought my first attempt was decent, ran it back a second time and struggled, and appreciated how my first attempt went even more. Second attempt raked me over the coals with how the MS and ST lined up with my player decks at the time. I dodged several bullets in the first attempt (avoiding wounds from Killmonger's Master Strikes early simply because Killmonger heroes hadn't come out of the Hero Deck yet etc.). My first game with What If, and I had fun lining up Liberate with Bystanders on Villains! Never got the What If keyword to synergize and didn't sweat it too much. Looking forward to more games with What If where I can focus on maximizing What If with things like Gambit, Wiccan, Wallcrawl, etc.! Fun setup and the Scheme helped cycle through the Heroes to see a lot of variety of options! My hero choices were to pair Apocalyptic Black Widow with Darkhawk and Bruce Banner with Killmonger (and a little Namora) with plenty of Tech cards from Bruce Banner to share between both decks. Fun time!
Realizing afterwards I got lucky with one of the scheme twists whiffing on turn 2 otherwise would have lost

Great setup.  Was crazy how often a Strike or Twist really disrupted my turns.  I found it really challenging balancing the Killmongers and trying to make sure I didn't lose too much to the cosmic rays.

column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Didn't enjoy! Had very tough enemies at the start of the game, my wife pulled through though for the win.  Not a fan of the keywords where there was a lot to track.

I'm not sure what was the goal here. Wounds came in pretty frequently from Masterstrikes, the Subterranea pushing escapes from the streets added to the Scheme loss condition. Not enough recruit to buy Killmonger cards before the betrayal made him switch teams. Namora contributed nothing to either game. I switched hero choices from War Machine and Hulkbuster Iron Man because WM didn't really work out. Bruce was an attempt to get more control over wounds and KO starter cards, but didn't show much in the second game. Captain Carter was okay. Would have loved to drop Namora in favor of HB I-M in a third game. Did everyone else have as many escapes? (I had 3 and 5 escapes in my games!)

I did get to use new heroes for me, so at least there's that. On to the bottom bracket I go!

Attempts 1 and 2 were totally different. In my first attempt Moon Knight's rare appeared very soon and got recruited by deck 1. This contributed to a 15 bystanders rescued difference between attempts 1 and 2. However, in attempt 1, all master Strikes occured and this had a hefty cost on both the total VP and the number of turns played. In both attempts Moon Knights's Instinct Common appeared and helped to KO a couple of starters. In attempt 1 however, the decks needed too many turns to hit Zemo (as the Master Strikes made him harder to hit). Wounds were gained from the first Master Strike and one of his Tactics, the other Wounds were preventable. In attempt 1 Deck 1 got a lot of rares, I think Ant-Man's, Stature's and Jocasta's on top of Moon Knight's. Despite those powerful cards, I could not KO that many starters with that deck, so it still felt pretty weak mostly.

Attempt 2 had a different start, where many more of Drax' cards showed up, and his rare was there from the start. I ended up getting a lot of starters KO'd in deck 2 and overall it was a much smoother game. Only 2 Master Strikes got played and both decks felt decent enough to get stuff done. Deck 1 was mostly Tech draw a card cards and 2 of Ant-Man's Uncommons together, which on luckier turns was enough to even defeat Zemo or just help out in the CIty. Deck 2 also had the majoirty of KO effects in attempt 2 and could get Drax's rare at one point, which made a lot of difference as I ended up with 32 attack in the final turn, due to doubling 16 attack (I had 4 of his artifacts and quite a few semi-strong cards that final turn, and only 3 starters left if I recall correctly), which made me able to first clear the City and get Zemo's last Tactic and hit his Final Blow.

In both attempts the first 5 Twists appeared before I had defeated any Sinister Six Villains. WIth 3 extra attack they were simply too hard to hit early on and if a Scheme Twist showed up, it ended up drawing another Scheme Twist sometimes, which in effect did very little. In attempt 1 Hobgoblin did get returned to the villain deck twice I believe to get me some extra bystanders again, but because that game took 32 turns, the PPT was lower than attempt 2. I found Stature's addition to the Hero Deck funny and midly frustrating, as her triggers could apply to almost no enemies (I mean the defeat a villain with 3 or less attack, that were only Mandroids, and even only if you had 1 or 0 in your Victory Pile). Her Uncommon could use them however, so that's a plus. There was a lot of Tech already available in Stature and Jocasta, so I ended up strengthening that with my Hero Choices Ant-Man and Moon Knight.

Jocasta's Empowered by Tech card could on occasion generate 6 attack (2 from her base strength and 4 from HQ), which helped in both games, but they ended up in the weaker deck both times. That all Hero cards had Wall-Crawl was a funny mechanic to play around, it felt balanced with the Sinister Six being +3 attack and helped to get either stronger attack turns or higher recruit turns with more information/certainty than you'd normally end up with. I was succesful in preventing escapes both attempts and didn't feel too overwhelmed, even when in attempt 2 3 Sinister Six Villains came out pretty soon without enough attack to Deal with them. I feel like the Master Strikes were the most impactful to the 'win condition' as they both slowed your chances at getting the Mastermind sooner and they lost you points.

Overall pretty fun to play, not too great of a challenge and the Wall-Crawl on everything had its charm. No great or powerful synergies either though, so not one of the  most fun games ever either. The consolation effect of getting easier Mandroid hits after a Master Strikes felt good too, but in attempt 1 I had hit a Thunderbird and no Mandroid yet, so I had to KO that one and that felt pretty bad. Attempt 2 had 1 Master Strike appear without any defeated villains and for the second both decks had saved up Mandroids, but luckily I was spared any more Master Strikes after that. Looking forward to week 3!

column36: High enemy power level, but our setup gave us also a great power level. Had much fun with Wall-Crawl, gained us early some cards for free. We picked Ant Man and Goliath to play everything around Stature and ignored Drax and Jocasta most of the time.

column36: WaspWoman and AntDude felt so good, that PowerPupa got to stay metamorphosing for the week. Lots of tech and strength synergy going around with copious amount of size-changing and everyone with wall crawl made for our quickest Legendary game in tournament play. Highlight was the last turn where Chameleon being placed in the city on a scheme twist meant fighting him could copy Drax's rare right below, allowing for two Zemo hits to close it out.
We forgot the wallcrawl ability in the first game during the first half of the game, so second game Ant-Man and Stature obliterated Zemo completely using wallcrawl-size changing-draw combination.

column36: This was a fun setup. I forgot how fun some of these early schemes are. It was tough to maintain VP as Zemo made you KO villains but by KOing mandroids I could keep their fight values low when they entered the city.
Better the second time picking Drax to focus on more to ramp up those attack artifacts as well getting Gamoras rare.

Despite the fun mechanics both game was weird. Scheme twists mostly appeared while no SS villains were defeated. On the other hand HQ got stuck with high cost cards. In the end pushed Zemo to 0 attack and cleared the city. Too bad for the one escape (one attack was missing for preventing that). Having only special sidekicks was new for me. Still with wall-crawling and phasing and moon knights powers some cool combos could be created.

First game, only 1 Black Knight showed up in the Villain Deck. I got Zemo down to the final blow but too many Twists happened early before and S6 got beaten so I lost turns. The second game went waaaayy better. I ended up getting to really use both my hero picks, and beat down a lot of s6.

This one was really fun. Mostly because of our hero pick. With Ant-Man and Goliath and all the size changing and wall crawl, it was a great ramp mechanic to clear out the city by turn 2 and was really no "waiting out the first 5 turns because attack is low" phase. You could really just get right into it.
Fun combination of scheme and mastermind, it was challenging but using antman with stature and jocasta made it possible to win

Both games were a lot of fun, but game 1 was better in every way. Infinity Gems took their sweet time to come out, so I never got Thanos below 18 Attack. Thankfully, Morbius was generating hands of 40+ Attack that could hit Thanos twice, even at this high level. P2 got a very trim deck with Snarling Fangs, but it was P1 spamming card-draw, Winds of Watoomb, Morbin’ Time and Insatiable Craving that did most of the work.
Fun combo of heroes and deck building possibilities. Getting the Soul Gem early in our second game was a big MVP.

Our first round we had an atrocious hq/starting hands/villain shuffle. All but 1 card in hq was 5 or 6 cost, we both had a 4 recruit, 2 flight hand AND all 5 of the first villain cards were either black order OR infinity gems

Second round we swapped who shuffled the hero deck and villain deck and that did the trick! I was also able to get the reality gem early which let us put two master strikes on the bottom of the deck, we were never in any huge danger and were able to both build solid decks.

Game 1 suffered from an early expensive HQ combined with an expensive City, so had 3 escapes. We won, mainly thanks to my wife who made 26 Recruit and 38 Attack to finish off Thanos. I had earlier taken 2 tactics in a turn thanks to the Infinity Crusher!
Game 2 seemed closer, but had fewer escapes. I had both Blade and Groot's Rares, my wife had achieved her objectives and KOd all her starters! We took down Thanos the old fashioned way, but in the same number of turns. This was fun!

This was one of the craziest games of Legendary I've ever played. After losing to the scheme pretty quickly in Attempt 1, I changed my strategy to keep Sentries out unless they threatened an escape.

In the 36 turns I played, not a SINGLE INFINTY GEM was revealed from the Villain deck (16 cards left.) I had to hit Thanos for 24 all 5 times. Luckily I had Gamora and Gamora, Attorneys at Brawl to help take him down.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: The first few rounds were very tense and I was constantly on the verge of a villain escape. The only way I was able to stop escapes was to let most of the villain HQ stay haunted. However, once I started getting better cards and a pile of shards for each player thanks to Groot I was able to stop Thanos from getting too many Darkhold pages pretty decisively. The Ancient One had good synergy with the other heroes, especially Morbius in this game. I really enjoyed using the Infinity Crusher, it was a great late game mechanic that let me take out two Mastermind tactics in one turn (while also throwing in a few shards for the second tactic fight).

This game was a 4.5 for fun - we enjoyed it - especially cos it had some of our fave heroes in it. Our first game - which we ended up using - had so many scheme twists come out early we thought we had no chance of winning but shards to the rescue for us. Our second game we took 2 more turns and scored a few more points but we're pretty sure the 2 turns will beat the .25 ppt. Great setup - nice and thematic, we loved beating Thanos to the ground.

Loved this week’s game. Only my 2nd game against Thanos and took a while to build up enough attack but managed to get there eventually. 2nd attempt didn’t fare so well as too many twists really early in the game.

Thanos is bulked...but Artifacts are powerful too. In addition to the Infinity Gems, Star-Lord's multiple Element Guns gave me all the ammo I needed later in the game. Gamora worked great with all the heroes so I felt confident with my two picks this week. A little snakebit with some of the villain cards meant I had to be patient in both of my playthrough attempts, but got the job done both times. Second attempt was 3 turns faster, but with an escape...so I went with my first for official scoring. The Astral Plane and Haunting actually helped me manage the city quite well. I could've done a better job leaving a cheap henchman available early on for when a scheme twist showed up. I felt I didn't take full advantage of those Cursed Pages. Overall, a really enjoyable setup.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: What a game! Thanos had the Darkhold in his grasp as he was defeated! Who better to take down The Mad Titan than his own daughter, the Deadliest Woman in the Universe? How about 2 of her! The fact that I had 0 escapes was a downright miracle. Between Haunts, the Astral Plane, and Salome ascending, I narrowly avoided multiple escapes. But both decks got cooking. The right side utilized the full power of the “Godslayer Blade” and “It’s Morbin’ Time” for some big turns. The right side went all in on The Ancient One, and with 2 copies of “War of the Mind” got 2 MM hits with recruit. Thanks for the set up!

GOTG Thanos continues to be my least favorite Mastermind because it's always a slog against him. The Fallen and Black Order somewhat helped with the easier rares and low attack but they also made getting the Gems much harder as a result.

Where as i went with captain carter and was able to help with support and the liberate function. Keeping up with defeating the locations was the key to success here.

column36: A lot of thought went into city management for this one, taking lesser kills so you could cover up villains in the victory pile. Lead to a lot of tactical thinking. I got lucky with the twists and only pulled one. One more turn would have been another.

We really enjoyed playing with all of the characters in the game. We loved learning about the shatter effect, soul binding effect and what if. lots of variety in the game the characters really meshed well together.

Good setup. Escapes only came early in bot games due to twist hitting before we could build the attack up.
M synergiesed with Wolfsbane great and Siryn with Gamora. We just had a small issue keeping it Moonlight consistently.

I wish the Zombie Group from Secret wars was added with Zombie Green Goblin.
I won both matchups.. I decided to go with my higher point per turn game with 1 escape rather than my less rounds to win 4 escape lower per game that I also won... I guess I'll find out.

Both games started with no rares, and both had 3 in the HQ by turn 10! In gamw 1 I was able to recruit the Captain Certer rare with hand 2 pretty eatly, and this let that hand scoop up the other rares in short order. Once Suryn starts shattering the Mastermind it's just a matter of time. In game 2 I won on the same turn but had much fewer VPs and 4 escapes.

First game I lost on turn 11 which was super disappointing.  But game two I managed to avoid that early loss and stave off defeat, though it took every available turn to do so.  I forgot how difficult Grim Reaper can be!  Player 1 focused mostly on Captain Carter, Wolfsbane and Gamora cards while Player 2 tried to leverage Multiple Man, with marginal success.

This was a really fun set up. I liked how the challenge wasn't how to get the W but instead how to get the W the most efficient way with the most points. I was able to win very quickly for me the first time in just 26 turns but that because I had to MM Shattering Rares that made Reaper very easy to hit so I just ignored the locations. My PPT was really bad that round so I kept my heroes and tried to focus the locations some too which almost doubled my PPT while only adding 3 turns. I sure hope those 3 turns don't bite me in the end.

Always love playing with Revelations! Doctor Strange Supreme felt like an obvious choice to deal with RotLD, but Locations can't be Soulbound and I struggled. Game 2 went much better, with Party Thor and Shatterstar ramping up really well together!
Great fun.  Very challenging.  The few amount of Twists made things all the more suspenseful.
Used what I like to call the Bystander Effect with Hiroim and Invisible Women's ability to get more bystanders and was able to send some heroes undercover to get even more points.

Ross is always a battle of attrition but we had the benefit of having Hiriom on our side for the fight. We used Invisible Woman to KO our deck relatively fast while also earning us the Bystanders we need to transform Hiriom's card and begin laying in on Ross and those pesky Helicopters. Our First attempt took 41 turns but our 2nd we were able to cut that time in half with a good villain deck and a quick assemble of our Exodia. 

We sadly never got the Hiroim rare in either game but Quake's rare came up twice, while not giving us a substantial amount of attack. It did allow us card selection in the HQ to continue our 4's deck and Bystander Deck to attack confidentially to ensure no escapes were to happen.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36:
The combo of Burrow with the Earthquake/Tsunami was very clever - you wanted to get someone into the Streets to allow you to truly kill Burrowing Villains, but that left them in jeopardy of being washed out by the Tsunami. Ra'ktar also made it risky to leave anyone in the Streets; he was responsible for one of my escapes.

I picked Thing to team up with Silver Surfer, trying to make a deck that produced a lot of Recruit and then sunk it into various Focus abilities. That worked OK, although I couldn't build it pure enough. I also was stuck with three 6-7 cost cards in the City for quite a while, which slowed things down a bit. I ended up getting Quake's Roil the Earth rare over there and actually used it in part to just clear cards I didn't want to buy out of the city. But Roil the Earth along with Tectonic Wave did provide the game-ending turn with 18 attack to hit Thunderbolt twice.

My other pick was She-hulk, which was kind of a hurried decision, but she did provide several more cards that triggered off of producing a lot of Recruit. So that deck ended up being pretty potent. The other deck I mostly picked Covert cards, notably Caiera's Shadowforged Blade, in a more traditional Attack-based deck. It worked out reasonably well, although oddly Silver Surfer's Warp Speed card (Focus 2 -> Draw a card) ended up over there and actually was pretty effective at least at moving through the deck faster.
went all-in on shield and was really fun!

I really enjoyed the game, but I did terrible. I picked Hiroim and Invisibile Woman because they let me KO things and they had nice Bystander effects. With the Helicopters I assumed Bystanders would be significant. In both games I had 3 escapes early on, but was able to stabilize. I didn't see Unseen Rescue in the first game, so I wasn't able to effectively farm bystanders. Second game I did get through the whole bystander deck, but the game ran for 44 turns, making it much worse score-wise than the first game.

column36: Pretty fun, once again love the burrow with the tide, got a cool blue deck going and the red deck was a big meme.

First game was tough. The city contracted right away and the HQ was full of high cost cards. I couldn't recruit anything into my deck to control the city and was wiped out in record time (13 turns). 
Second game went better. After 7 or 8 turns I thought I was looking at a repeat, but She-Hulk was able to hurl enough trucks to turn the tide (pun intended). That gave me a breather and I was able to work on taking down General Ross/ Red Hulk. 
Not a complaint, but the swingy nature of the setup and being saddled with Silver Surfer (who is boring) pushed the fun score down a bit.

Having Burrow with the Tsunami wiping out the streets was both fun and tricky (risk killing them once to move to streets and risk escaping or wait to the move but have a build up if there ends up being only 3 city locations).  I did find the Mastermind was difficult in that mine flipped early and trying to hit for 9 early on was difficult and could not take on the helicopters.  Overall enjoyed.

Was inches away, final scheme twist came out the turn I would have taken down Ross. Super Frustrating. My first attempt I had to throw out cause I misread the Scheme and didn't pull again when it flipped back, which made this near loss sting even harder. I had a solid Shield deck and a solid Recruit deck by the end of it, but the HQ didn't line up early enough for me to be quick enough to win :(

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Got a lucky turn 1 rest to get rid of 2 wounds and then shortly after pick up Psilords rare. After that Psilord controlled the villain deck while M controlled the mastermind. Very fun set up will but so much shuffling.

Kudos again -- lots of fun.  In the second game (didn't use), by turn 11 I had already flipped all the master strikes and four twists.  I really thought I had shuffled a ton -- I guess just random luck -- though that did allow me to burn off most of those starting wounds.

Those Mr. Sinister Clones did add another level of decision making when it came time to decide if I wanted to fight the MM or clear space from the city.  Had to try to keep at least two spaces open.

I really liked the Scheme that had me start with Heroes/Wounds in the player decks -- really helped streamline strategies between the two players.
Lots of good combos between the heroes. The scheme was not difficult at all with lots of ways to deal with wounds. Mysterio was cake once we got rolling.

The clone mechanic really helped with recruits. Felt the Sin Six were a little underpowered against the matchups due to the negatives most get from different game states and the ability to build deck up quickly between investigate and clone. Very fun and did much better second game when I was able to get one deck stacked on with M and the Sisters.

We tried to get lucky on the first game with Psi-Lord, but that didn't work out. The hand with Psi-Lord could rarely get enough attack to hit Misterio and the game took too long. 
Second game went much better as the hand with F4 United could easily KO wounds and Agents, while the other one had tons of draw from MM and Strong Man investigations.

That was pretty fun.  I gave serious thought to taking two SHIELD Heroes as my picks, to take advantage of the Hydra Elite group and get those sweet UC points, but I figured that wouldn't give me enough wound ablation, and it's early enough in the tournament that I'm betting on SHIELD coming up again.  Pretty happy with my picks, although in the first game (the one I submitted) I got unlucky and couldn't get any deck cull going, so it took a lot longer to get up to speed than I hoped.  Second game might have gone better but the Twists just game too fast and I never really stood a chance.

I really liked the mastermind-scheme-henchmen combo, they were all fun to play! Played two difficult games, lost the first one, won the second one. The one thing I kinda didn't like was the fact that I played all of the game components recently, was hoping to play with a few newer sets also. Maybe next week!

First game I got a ton of early wounds due to scheme twists. I was finally able to turn it around to finish off Mysterio at the end. One of his tactics was super useful to send the final scheme twist to the bottom of the villain deck. The second game I started off a lot better, but then my decks just floundered there, and I was not able to beat Mysterio before the final scheme appeared.

First time playing Hela.
Thrown artifacts were awesome, Hela was much easier to fight without her villain group and the scheme was easy to control.

First game was rough. Getting early escapes due to the scheme is just about impossible to circumvent without allowing more escapes to balance out the Kree/Skrulls in the escape pile. 
Second game was much better. Logan and Okoye had much more attack starting out which gave us a better start. But those early escapes still were unfortunate. Oh well. Hela was never really a concern, since keeping the city clear of Kree/Skrulls meant that she was never boosted outside of her tactics putting Villains directly in those spots.

The scheme was fun! The heros that were given weren't bad together at all. The one hero White wolf didn't work great but still succeeded.  It was super close to being defeated. The mastermind was tricky! Can't wait for the next setup!

Fun but a bit too swingy a setup for my taste. Black Panthers worked well with Namor. Not as much with Queen Storm which was surprising. Feel like you really would have to build around her to be successful.
